title: HTML Basics
description: Fundamental concepts of HTML structure and elements.

questions.question: What does HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language. It is the standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser.

questions.question: Which tag is used to create a hyperlink?
questions.explanation: The <a> tag (anchor tag) is used to define a hyperlink.

questions.question: What is the correct HTML element for the largest heading?
questions.explanation: <h1> defines the most important heading. <h6> defines the least important heading.

questions.question: Which character is used to indicate an end tag?
questions.explanation: End tags are distinguished by a slash (/) before the tag name.

questions.question: Inline elements are normally displayed without starting a new line.
questions.explanation: Inline elements like <span> or <a> do not start on a new line and only take up as much width as necessary.

questions.question: How can you open a link in a new tab/browser window?
questions.explanation: target='_blank' specifies that the linked document will open in a new tab or window.

questions.question: Which HTML attribute specifies an alternate text for an image, if the image cannot be displayed?
questions.explanation: The alt attribute provides alternative text for an image if the user for some reason cannot view it.

questions.question: Which doctype is correct for HTML5?
questions.explanation: HTML5 doctype is simple: <!DOCTYPE html>.

title: CSS Styling
description: Learn how to style your web pages with CSS.

questions.question: What does CSS stand for?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets.

questions.question: Which property is used to change the background color?
questions.explanation: background-color sets the background color of an element.

questions.question: How do you select an element with id 'demo'?
questions.explanation: The id selector uses the hash (#) character.

questions.question: Which CSS property is used to control the text size?
questions.explanation: font-size sets the size of the font.

questions.question: How do you make each word in a text start with a capital letter?
questions.explanation: text-transform: capitalize capitalizes the first character of each word.

questions.question: Which property is used to change the left margin of an element?
questions.explanation: margin-left sets the left margin of an element.

questions.question: What is the default value of the position property?
questions.explanation: static is the default position value. Elements render in order, as they appear in the document flow.

questions.question: Which keyword is used for 'flexbox' layout?
questions.explanation: display: flex initiates a flex container.

title: JavaScript Core
description: Master the logic of the web.

questions.question: Inside which HTML element do we put the JavaScript?
questions.explanation: The <script> tag is used to define client-side JavaScript.

questions.question: What is the correct syntax for referring to an external script called 'xxx.js'?
questions.explanation: The src attribute specifies the URL of an external script file.

questions.question: How do you write 'Hello World' in an alert box?
questions.explanation: alert() displays a dialog box with an optional message.

questions.question: How do you create a function in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: function name() {} is the standard way to declare a function.

questions.question: How does a FOR loop start?
questions.explanation: Standard for loop syntax: for (initialization; condition; increment).

questions.question: What is the correct way to write a JavaScript array?
questions.explanation: Array literals use square brackets [].

questions.question: Which event occurs when the user clicks on an HTML element?
questions.explanation: onclick occurs when the user clicks on an element.

questions.question: How do you declare a JavaScript variable?
questions.explanation: var, let, or const are used to declare variables.

questions.question: What is React?
options: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces
questions.explanation: React is a declarative, efficient, and flexible JavaScript library for building user interfaces.

questions.question: Which method is not part of the React component lifecycle?
questions.explanation: componentDidInit is not a valid lifecycle method in React.

questions.question: What is a 'prop' in React?
options: An argument passed into a React component
questions.explanation: Props (properties) are arguments passed into React components.

questions.question: What hook is used to handle side effects in functional components?
questions.explanation: useEffect lets you perform side effects (data fetching, subscriptions) in function components.

questions.question: What is the virtual DOM?
options: A direct copy of the real DOM
A lightweight copy of the DOM kept in memory
questions.explanation: The virtual DOM is a lightweight JavaScript representation of the DOM used to optimize rendering.

questions.question: Which command is used to create a new React app?
questions.explanation: npx create-react-app my-app is the standard command (though Vite is now preferred).

questions.question: How do you handle events in React?
options: Using inline event handlers with camelCase
questions.explanation: React events are named using camelCase, rather than lowercase.

questions.question: What is JSX?
questions.explanation: JSX stands for JavaScript XML. It allows you to write HTML-like code inside JavaScript.

questions.question: What does SQL stand for?
questions.explanation: SQL stands for Structured Query Language.

questions.question: Which SQL statement is used to extract data from a database?
questions.explanation: The SELECT statement is used to select data from a database.

questions.question: Which SQL statement is used to update data in a database?
questions.explanation: The UPDATE statement is used to modify the existing records in a table.

questions.question: Which SQL statement is used to delete data from a database?
questions.explanation: The DELETE statement is used to delete existing records in a table.

questions.question: Which SQL statement is used to insert new data in a database?
questions.explanation: The INSERT INTO statement is used to insert new records in a table.

questions.question: With SQL, how do you select all the columns from a table named 'Persons'?
questions.explanation: SELECT * FROM TableName selects all columns.

questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter records?
questions.explanation: The WHERE clause is used to filter records.

questions.question: Which SQL keyword is used to sort the result-set?
questions.explanation: The ORDER BY keyword is used to sort the result-set in ascending or descending order.

questions.question: What does NoSQL stand for?
questions.explanation: NoSQL encompasses a wide variety of different database technologies, standing for 'Not Only SQL'.

questions.question: Which of these is a popular NoSQL database?
questions.explanation: MongoDB is a widely used document-oriented NoSQL database.

questions.question: In MongoDB, what are records called?
questions.explanation: Data in MongoDB receives a flexible JSON-like format called Documents.

questions.question: Which CAP theorem property is often sacrificed in NoSQL systems for high availability?
questions.explanation: Many NoSQL databases sacrifice strong Consistency for Availability and Partition Tolerance (AP systems).

questions.question: What format does MongoDB use to store documents?
questions.explanation: MongoDB uses BSON (Binary JSON) to store data.

questions.question: NoSQL databases are primarily designed for:
questions.explanation: NoSQL is built for horizontal scaling (sharding) to handle large volumes of data.

questions.question: Which type of NoSQL database is Redis?
questions.explanation: Redis is a high-performance in-memory Key-Value store.

questions.question: Are schemas mandatory in NoSQL?
options: No, they are schema-less or schema-flexible
questions.explanation: NoSQL databases allow you to insert data without a predefined schema.

questions.question: Which data type is used to create a variable that should store text?
questions.explanation: String is the class used to represent text in Java.

questions.question: How do you create a variable with the numeric value 5?
questions.explanation: int is the data type for integers.

questions.question: Which method is the entry point for any Java program?
questions.explanation: public static void main(String[] args) is the entry point.

questions.question: How do you create an object of a class named MyClass?
questions.explanation: The 'new' keyword is used to instantiate a class.

questions.question: Which operator is used to compare two values?
questions.explanation: == is the equality operator.

questions.question: To declare an array in Java, define the variable type with:
questions.explanation: Brackets [] are used for arrays (e.g., int[] arr).

questions.question: Which keyword is used to inherit a class?
questions.explanation: extends is used for class inheritance.

questions.question: What is the size of an int variable?
questions.explanation: A Java int is a 32-bit signed two's complement integer.

questions.question: What is Next.js built on top of?
questions.explanation: Next.js is a React framework for production.

questions.question: Which function is used for Static Site Generation (SSG)?
questions.explanation: getStaticProps fetches data at build time.

questions.question: How do you create a dynamic route in Next.js?
questions.explanation: Dynamic segments are defined by wrapping a folder or file name in square brackets: [folderName].

questions.question: Which component is used for client-side navigation?
questions.explanation: Next.js provides a <Link> component for client-side transitions.

questions.question: Where should static assets like images be placed?
questions.explanation: The public directory is used to serve static assets.

questions.question: What is the file name for the custom error page?
questions.explanation: _error.js handles server errors. 404.js handles page not found.

questions.question: How do you enable CSS modules?
questions.explanation: Next.js supports CSS Modules using the [name].module.css file naming convention.

questions.question: Which function runs on every request in Next.js?
questions.explanation: getServerSideProps runs on every request for SSR.

questions.question: Angular is a framework based on which language?
questions.explanation: Angular is built with and optimized for TypeScript.

questions.question: Which directive is used for two-way data binding?
questions.explanation: ng-model binds the value of HTML controls to application data.

questions.question: What is a decorator in Angular?
options: A function that adds metadata to a class
questions.explanation: Decorators (like @Component) attach metadata to classes so Angular knows how to create them.

questions.question: Which CLI command generates a new component?
questions.explanation: ng generate component (or ng g c) creates a new component.

questions.question: What is Dependency Injection in Angular?
options: A design pattern for supplying dependencies to a class
questions.explanation: DI allows classes to receive dependencies from an external source rather than creating them.

questions.question: Which symbol is used for property binding?
questions.explanation: Square brackets [] are used for property binding (e.g., [src]).

questions.question: Which symbol is used for event binding?
questions.explanation: Parentheses () are used for event binding (e.g., (click)).

questions.question: What is the purpose of NgModule?
questions.explanation: NgModules configure the injector and the compiler and help organize related code.
